title: MARION COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY - 211info
text: MARION COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY MARION COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Marion County Housing Authority (MCHA), under contract with the US Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), administers the Section 8 Voucher Program and several other rental assistance programs. MCHA also owns and manages several affordable apartment complexes for seniors, families, and farm laborers. Serves the rural areas of Marion County, which are outside the Urban Growth Boundary of the City of Salem. No assistance is available for Salem/Keizer residents. 2645 Portland Road NE Suite 200 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Eligibility varies by program. HUD-established income limits apply. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm, closed federal holidays Email (503) 798-4170 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 798-4170 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, or visit website FEES: Rent amounts vary by program DOCUMENTS: Proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Rural Marion County, outside the Urban Growth Boundary of the Cities of Salem/Keizer (Cities of Aumsville, Aurora, Brooks, Detroit, Donald, Gates, Gervais, Idanha, Jefferson, Lyons, Marion, Mehama, Mill City, Monitor, Mount Angel, Saint Paul, Scotts Mills, Silverton, Stayton, Sublimity, Turner, and Woodburn) TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at the corner of Portland Road NE and Pine Street NE Services: Elderly/Disabled Home Rental Listings Housing Complaints * Public Housing Residents Housing Complaints * Section 8 Housing Residents Public Housing Rural Housing Development Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers Section 8/Rental Assistance Program Rental Listings Subsidized Home Acquisition Subsidized Housing Administrative Organizations If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
